sourceRef.citation: Kourovka Notebook, Problem 17.108 (Khukhro & Mazurov eds., ed.17)

statement.latex: Is the group$a,b,t a^t=ab,\;\, b^t=ba$linear? If not, this would be an easy example of a non-linear hyperbolic group. { M.\,Sapir} Yes, this group is linear. Indeed, as noticed by M.\,Sapir, this group is the mapping torus of an irreducible atoroidal self-monomorphism of a free group; thus it is virtually special, and hence$$-linear by Theorem~B in (M.\,F.\,Hagen, D.\,T.\,Wise, Duke Math.~J., { 16...

reiAssessment.solveRationale: Group theory VI_BRIDGING; Rei not specialized in group theory (yet)
reiAssessment.formalizationNotes: Kourovka problems are typically research-level group theory
